guide.title: Quick guide
guide.manual_subtitle: Key points to protect your library, organize content, and use advanced features.
guide.artist_sort_tip: On each artist page you can sort songs by name, artist, time added, size, plays, duration, or last played.

library.title: Library and backup
library.subtitle: Where your content lives and how to prevent data loss.

tips.1: Listenfy stores files, covers, playlists, and metadata inside the app's private storage.
tips.2: Before uninstalling or clearing data, create a ZIP backup from Data and downloads.
tips.3: A full backup can take a while on large libraries because it includes files and metadata.
tips.4: If you include variants, versions such as 8D, instrumental, or processed files are also saved.

tips.1: The audio player keeps the queue, miniplayer, favorites, repeat, and shuffle playback.
tips.2: If you close the miniplayer while a song is playing, Listenfy keeps the queue so you can continue later; if you pause first and then close it, that session is not restored when the app opens again.
tips.3: In the full player you can tap back or swipe down to minimize and return to the miniplayer.
tips.4: If you notice latency or a brief cut when changing songs, pause first and then choose the next track; this helps especially with large queues, heavy files, or Connect active.
tips.5: Crossfade, equalizer, sleep timer, and default volume live in Settings > Audio.
tips.6: Clean sound detects long silences and lets you trim only the segments you choose; it keeps the main metadata from the original song.
tips.7: 8D and instrumental modes require the external backend; when finished, they are saved as reusable offline variants.

editable_home.title: Editable home
editable_home.subtitle: Customize which widgets appear on Home.

tips.1: On Home, tap the edit home icon in the top bar to open the editor in a separate window.
tips.2: You can enable, disable, and reorder My favorites, For you today, Most played, Recently played, Featured, Latest imports, To listen, and Random mix.
tips.3: Compatible widgets can switch between card and list views; the editor shows the chosen mode before saving.
tips.4: You can also add combined sections for Artists, Playlists, and Collections; each one keeps its view mode.
tips.5: In combined sections, long-press an item to remove it without rebuilding the whole widget.
tips.6: Expanded widget views show the sort button next to the grid/list switch; Home stays clean and only opens the section.
tips.7: Long-pressing an audio or video inside an editable widget makes Select multiple open the expanded module view with the first item already selected.
tips.8: In the expanded view you can delete, share through external apps, or send through Listenfy transfer without leaving the widget flow.
tips.9: Sorting is saved per widget: Latest imports, Most played, and Recently played use their own criteria; Favorites, Featured, and For you today allow more parameters.
tips.10: Tap Save to apply changes. Reset returns to the default layout inside the editor.
tips.11: In video mode, widgets that only apply to audio, such as For you today, are hidden automatically.

wrapped.title: Wrapped and stats
wrapped.subtitle: Listening summary, imports, and local trends.

tips.1: From Data and downloads you can open Check your stats to see your Wrapped-style summary.
tips.2: Music metrics separate songs, favorites, artists, Atlas regions, completed tracks, and early skips.
tips.3: Import metrics separate songs and videos, show most downloaded artists, top imports, strongest month, and strongest week.
tips.4: Regions depend on country and region metadata used by Atlas; the more consistent it is, the better the summary gets.
tips.5: Exporting and restoring the ZIP also keeps the metadata that powers these stats.

artists.title: Artists and collaborations
artists.subtitle: Metadata needed for Atlas and relationships.

tips.1: From Artists you can open Listenfly Atlas.
tips.2: Atlas works best when you edit artists and define main region, country, and artist type.
tips.3: Song, artist, playlist, and themed-list covers can come from a local file or web search.
tips.4: If a collaboration should count for multiple artists, write it in Artist with patterns such as ft., feat., featuring, or with.
tips.5: After the collaboration marker, separate guests with commas, x, or &: Artist ft. Guest1, Guest2 & Guest3.
tips.6: If the title suggests feat or ft but the Artist field does not reflect it, saving shows a warning so you can fix it.
tips.7: On each artist page you can sort songs by name, artist, time added, size, plays, duration, or last played.

tips.1: Open Atlas from the Listenfly Atlas card inside Artists.
tips.2: Atlas does not guess everything by itself: it depends on the region and country you have on artists and songs.
tips.3: If you close the miniplayer, Continue should try to resume the locally saved station, song, and position.
tips.4: If app data, cookies, or internal storage are cleared, saved sessions can be lost.
tips.5: Local recommendations improve as your library gets more consistent metadata.

tips.1: Collections organize videos with their own cover and color.
tips.2: From Collections you can open Captures; you no longer need to find that feature as a separate module.
tips.3: Use search and sorting when a Collection grows too large.
tips.4: A good structure is Collection > child Collection > videos, for example Movies and series > Anime > Season 1.
tips.5: Collections are manual: if a video does not appear, add it to the corresponding Collection.

tips.1: Captures taken from the video player appear in Captures, inside Collections.
tips.2: Each capture keeps name, weight, capture date, optional tag, and source, meaning the video it came from.
tips.3: Each capture menu uses the same flow as audio and video: Edit opens the shared data page to change name and multiple tags.
tips.4: When using a capture as a cover, the picker shows videos and Collections with search, chips, and visual selection like the add-items flow.
tips.5: When you create a tag, Listenfy treats it like a collection: it can have its own name, color, and thumbnail.
tips.6: Tag folders also use the shared edit page, with name, content, color, and thumbnail chosen from their captures.
tips.7: Color and thumbnail are saved per tag: if you use the same tag on multiple captures, they all share the same visual collection.
tips.8: The folders button opens the tag view, where each tag appears as a Finder-style folder with a counter, cover, and color dot.
tips.9: Tapping a tag folder opens Captures filtered by that tag.

video.title: Video
video.subtitle: Gestures and tools in the video player.

tips.1: Double tap the left or right half of the video to rewind or fast-forward seconds.
tips.2: A vertical one-finger drag changes volume; a two-finger drag changes speed.
tips.3: Double tap with two fingers toggles play and pause.
tips.4: When moving the progress bar, a preview can appear; you can also save a capture from the camera icon.
tips.5: On Android, if the video keeps playing when you leave, it can enter PiP automatically.
tips.6: Video cards can show status labels such as Pending, Continue watching, Watched, or Completed depending on saved progress.
tips.7: From Settings > Video you can hide those labels on all videos or only on short videos of 13 minutes or less.
tips.8: Hiding labels only changes the view: it does not delete progress, saved positions, or stats.

connect.title: Connect and transfers
connect.subtitle: Remote control, imports, and offline sharing.

tips.1: Listenfy Connect opens a URL or QR so you can control playback from another device on the same network.
tips.2: With very large queues, Connect can feel slower because it has to sync more state.
tips.3: If a remote track change feels delayed, pause from the phone or from Connect before choosing another song.
tips.4: You can import URLs or files from Android's Share menu.
tips.5: Audio or video multi-selection can share several files through external apps up to 300 MB total.
tips.6: Multi-selection can also be sent through internal Listenfy transfer with metadata and a 1 GB total limit.
tips.7: If a selection exceeds the allowed limit, Listenfy keeps the selection and shows the warning before starting the send.
tips.8: P2P transfer uses QR to send files and metadata without depending on the internet when the device allows it.
tips.9: Data transfer between songs moves title, artist, cover, lyrics, favorites, stats, and playlists to another version without changing the destination file.
tips.10: After transferring data, you can delete the previous version from the library or keep it.

limits.title: Known limits
limits.subtitle: Behaviors that are still being polished.

tips.1: Libraries with more than 400 or 500 items can make a screen slower if every element loads at once.
tips.2: Exporting a ZIP can take a long time; restoring is usually faster.
tips.3: Some videos may not report the correct duration if their metadata is incomplete.
tips.4: If certain downloads fail, update cookies.txt from Data and downloads.
